candidateFor: Adults with a diagnosed deviated nasal septum causing chronic difficulty breathing through one or both nostrils
Patients with persistent nasal obstruction that has not responded to nasal sprays, antihistamines, or other non-surgical treatments
Those experiencing recurrent sinus infections, snoring, or disrupted sleep linked to a structural septal deformity
Patients whose deviated septum was caused by facial trauma or a previous nasal injury
International patients considering combining septoplasty with turbinate reduction or functional rhinoplasty in a single surgical visit
Non-smokers in general good health who can commit to the recommended 10–14 day in-country recovery period

whatToExpect.detail: An ENT specialist or rhinoplasty surgeon examines your nasal passages, reviews your symptom history, and may order imaging to confirm the degree and location of the septal deviation. Pre-operative blood work and anesthesia clearance are completed at this stage. International patients typically arrive one to two days before surgery to allow time for these assessments.

Surgery is performed entirely through the nostrils — no external incisions in most cases. Under general anesthesia or deep sedation with local anesthetic, the surgeon repositions or removes the deviated cartilage and bone. Turbinate reduction is frequently added in the same session. Most patients are discharged the same day once recovery from anesthesia is confirmed.

Internal splints or packing are removed around day 7 at a follow-up clinic visit. Mild swelling, light nasal discharge, and some congestion are normal in the first week. Light daily activity typically resumes within one week; strenuous exercise and nose-blowing are restricted longer. Full internal healing of the septum takes several months, though breathing improvement is often noticed much sooner.

whyKorea: Korean ENT surgeons and rhinoplasty specialists train extensively in combined functional and structural nasal procedures, and many clinics in Seoul's medical districts handle high volumes of both domestic and international septal cases each year

Korea's Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS) regulates surgical facilities and equipment standards, and internationally accredited hospitals provide the same quality framework international patients would expect at home

Self-pay costs for septoplasty in Korea run approximately $2,400–$5,500, which represents a meaningful reduction compared to out-of-pocket prices for the same procedure in the United States, United Kingdom, or Australia

Korea's established medical tourism infrastructure — including English-speaking coordinators, international patient centers at major hospitals, and streamlined pre-operative testing — reduces logistical burden for patients traveling from Africa, the GCC, or Southeast Asia

faqs.question: Will septoplasty change how my nose looks from the outside?
faqs.answer: Septoplasty is a functional procedure and in most cases produces no visible external change. The surgeon works entirely inside the nostrils. If you also want to alter the shape of the nose, that requires a separate cosmetic rhinoplasty component, which some patients combine with septoplasty in the same surgical session.

faqs.question: How long do I actually need to stay in Korea after the surgery?
faqs.answer: The recommended stay is 10–14 days. This covers pre-operative testing, the procedure itself, the day-7 splint removal appointment, and a final check confirming you are safe to fly. Leaving before splints are removed or before a physician clears you for air travel is not advisable.

faqs.question: What is the approximate cost of septoplasty in Korea for a self-paying international patient?
faqs.answer: Typical self-pay costs range from approximately $2,400 to $5,500 USD. The final figure depends on whether turbinate reduction or other procedures are performed at the same time, the facility type, and anesthesia fees. Obtain itemized quotes from at least two clinics before committing.

faqs.question: Is septoplasty performed under general anesthesia or local anesthesia?
faqs.answer: Most Korean surgeons perform septoplasty under general anesthesia or deep sedation combined with local anesthetic, so patients are comfortable and still throughout the procedure. The choice depends on the complexity of the case and the patient's medical history, and will be confirmed at the pre-operative consultation.

faqs.question: Can septoplasty be combined with other nasal procedures during the same visit to Korea?
faqs.answer: Yes. Turbinate reduction is commonly performed alongside septoplasty in the same surgical session. Some patients also combine functional septoplasty with cosmetic rhinoplasty. Combining procedures means one anesthesia event and one recovery period, which can be practical for international patients with a fixed travel window. Discuss your goals at consultation so the surgical plan can be finalized before your arrival.
